behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: The failure is in AI/ML output quality: whether the real speech-to-text model preserves diarization/speaker labels. A mocked transcript would hide this. Rejected lower adjacent level (`System`): behavior asks for label preservation quality ("preserves speaker labels"), not just API transport/runtime behavior. Rejected higher adjacent level (`Workflow`): this behavior targets one model judgment at transcription time, not multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real audio through the real speech-to-text API and assert transcript segments include correct speaker labels for known attendees on a labeled fixture meeting; fail if labels are missing/collapsed/misattributed.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic parsing/structuring logic at a local code boundary. No real OS/network/model behavior is required to trigger the failure. Rejected adjacent level (`Integration`): behavior text is local transformation ("parsing extracts ... into the ... structure"), which can fail within one parser/mapping function without cross-module I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function transcript lines with explicit assignments (e.g., "Alex: Priya, send the contract") and assert extracted objects contain correct `assignee` and `task_text` fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on what the real summarization LLM decides to include; stubbed outputs would always pass and hide omission failures. Rejected lower adjacent level (`System`): this is content coverage quality ("contains at least one action item for every attendee"), not merely network/process behavior. Rejected higher adjacent level (`Workflow`): assertion is on one summary-generation outcome, not a required multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript inputs with known per-attendee assignments, call the real summarization model, and assert the produced action-item list covers each assigned attendee at least once.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: The behavior is deterministic routing/field-mapping logic after decisions are already identified. No real infra or model output quality is needed for this specific failure boundary. Rejected adjacent level (`Integration`): wording is local mapping behavior ("routes ... into the `decisions` section schema") that can be validated at function/object level.
behaviors.test_description: Given an in-memory intermediate object containing identified decisions plus narrative text, assert mapper places decisions only in `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model judgment and output placement quality: the real LLM must detect key decisions and place them in the correct section. Mocked outputs would mask misses/misplacement. Rejected lower adjacent level (`System`): requirement is semantic placement quality ("appear ... in `decisions` section, not buried"), not infra reliability. Rejected higher adjacent level (`Workflow`): can be validated on a single real summarization call result.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcripts containing explicit decisions through the real summarization call and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` entries and absent as decision-only mentions in narrative.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly multi-step orchestration with sequential segment processing and merge coherence, spanning repeated model calls and handoffs. Rejected lower adjacent level (`Agentic`): behavior text requires chained steps ("splits ... into segments", "processes them sequentially", "merging the results").
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, execute end-to-end segmented processing with real model calls per segment and final merge, then assert all segments were processed in order and merged output remains structurally coherent and complete.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is a full end-to-end agent pipeline over real inputs and multiple AI-dependent stages (transcription + summarization) with sequential handoffs. Rejected lower adjacent level (`Agentic`): scope is explicitly pipeline-wide ("full pipeline ... completes without manual intervention"), not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run production-like pipeline on a real meeting recording from upload through final structured delivery, asserting unattended completion, valid final schema, and no human recovery steps.
